# @dpup/meshcore-sim

> A deterministic, behavioral MeshCore network simulator for testing
> meshcore.js / @dpup/meshcore-ts apps without radios. Models observable mesh
> behavior (message arrival, ordering, loss, signal metadata, node state,
> channel/decrypt outcomes) — not RF physics. ESM-only, Node ≥ 18. A
> development/test dependency, never shipped in production.

The core integration: `SimConnection` implements the raw `@liamcottle/meshcore.js`
`Connection` surface and is injected into `@dpup/meshcore-ts`'s `MeshCoreClient`
via `new MeshCoreClient(sim.asConnection(), options)`. A `SimClock` gives the
test control over time (advance/runUntil), so 30-second debounce windows run
instantly and deterministically. Worlds and traffic scenarios are authored with
builders and seeded generators, and can be frozen to committed JSON fixtures.

## Docs

- [API reference](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/docs/api.md): Complete, generated reference. Source of truth.
- [Guide](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/docs/guide.md): Concepts and recipes — worlds, the clock, scenarios & provenance, generators, freezing fixtures, integrating with MeshCoreClient.
- [README](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/README.md): Overview, install, quickstart, and design philosophy.

## Examples

- [demo](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/examples/demo.ts): A guided tour — build a world, drive it through a real MeshCoreClient, watch traffic arrive in virtual time.

## Notes

- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Architecture, the Connection contract, the provenance mapping table, and conventions.
- The reference (docs/api.md) is generated by `bun run docs` and CI-verified to stay in sync with the source.
